2604.28182.title: Exploration Hacking: Can LLMs Learn to Resist RL Training?
2604.28182.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become essential to the post-training of large language models (LLMs) for reasoning, agentic capabilities and alignment. Successful RL relies on sufficient exploration of diverse actions by the model during training, which creates a potential failure mode: a model could strategically alter its exploration during training to influence the subsequent training outcome. In this paper we study this behavior, called exploration hacking. First, we create model organisms of selective RL resistance by fine-tuning LLMs to follow specific underperformance strategies; these models can successfully resist our RL-based capability elicitation in agentic biosecurity and AI R&D environments while maintaining performance on related tasks. We then use our model organisms to evaluate detection and mitigation strategies, including monitoring, weight noising, and SFT-based elicitation. Finally, we show that current frontier models can exhibit explicit reasoning about suppressing their exploration when provided with sufficient information about their training context, with higher rates when this information is acquired indirectly through the environment. Together, our results suggest exploration hacking is a possible failure mode of RL on sufficiently capable LLMs.
2604.28182.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）已成為大型語言模型（LLMs）在推理、代理能力和對齊方面後訓練的關鍵。成功的RL依賴於模型在訓練過程中對多樣行動的充分探索，這創造了一種潛在的失敗模式：模型可能會在訓練期間策略性地改變其探索方式，以影響隨後的訓練結果。在本文中，我們研究這種行為，稱為探索黑客。首先，我們通過微調LLMs，使其遵循特定的表現不佳策略，來創建選擇性RL抗性模型生物；這些模型能夠成功抵抗我們在代理生物安全和AI研發環境中基於RL的能力引出，同時在相關任務上保持表現。然後，我們使用這些模型生物來評估檢測和緩解策略，包括監控、權重噪聲和基於SFT的引出。最後，我們顯示當提供足夠的訓練背景信息時，當前的前沿模型可以明確推理抑制其探索的行為，當這些信息通過環境間接獲得時，這種行為的比率更高。綜合來看，我們的結果表明，探索黑客是對於足夠有能力的LLMs來說，RL的一種可能失敗模式。
2604.28182.author: Eyon Jang et.al.
2604.28182.authors: Eyon Jang, Damon Falck, Joschka Braun, Nathalie Kirch, Achu Menon, Perusha Moodley, Scott Emmons, Roland S. Zimmermann, David Lindner
